sections.title: Genitive for Possession
sections.explanation: The genitive case answers 'whose?' (чий? чия? чиє?). It shows that something belongs to someone or something. Masculine nouns ending in a consonant add -а or -у. Feminine nouns change -а to -и/-і. Neuter nouns change -о to -а, -е to -я.

exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -и in the genitive: сестра → сестри.

exercises.hint: Masculine nouns add -а in the genitive.
exercises.explanation: Брат → брата. Most masculine animate nouns take -а in the genitive case.

exercises.prompt: Put the word in the genitive case:
exercises.explanation: Мама → мами. Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -и in the genitive.

exercises.prompt: Match the nominative form to its genitive:
exercises.explanation: Masculine animate: -а; feminine -а → -и; neuter -о → -а.
Дівчинка → дівчинки. After к, the feminine genitive ending is -и.
The genitive of тато is тата, not тату. Тату is the dative form.
Будинок → будинку. Some masculine inanimate nouns take -у in the genitive (buildings, locations).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: Вчитель → вчителя. Masculine nouns ending in -ь change to -я in the genitive.
The possessor in the genitive (вчителя) follows the noun it modifies (книга).
Подруга → подруги (-а → -и); батько → батька (-о → -а); море → моря (-е → -я); сусід → сусіда (add -а); книга → книги (-а → -и).

sections.title: Genitive for Absence
sections.explanation: The genitive case is used with немає (there is no / there isn't) to express absence. The noun that is absent goes into the genitive case. Немає + genitive = 'there is no...'.

exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns: -о → -а in the genitive. Молоко → молока.
Час → часу. Abstract and collective masculine nouns typically take -у in the genitive.
Вода → води. Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -и in the genitive.
After немає, the noun must be in the genitive case: хліб → хліба.
Вчителька → вчительки. Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -и after к.
Цукор → цукру. Substance/material nouns take -у in the genitive.
Немає requires the genitive case: студенти → студентів (genitive plural).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: Гроші → грошей (genitive plural). Немає always takes the genitive.

exercises.prompt: Match each noun to its genitive form used with немає:
exercises.explanation: Сіль → солі (fem -ь → -і); хліб → хліба (masc animate-style); кава → кави (-а → -и); чай → чаю (substance); м'ясо → м'яса (-о → -а).
Сонце → сонця. Neuter nouns ending in -це change to -ця in the genitive.

sections.title: Genitive After Prepositions
sections.explanation: Several important prepositions require the genitive case: від (from), до (to/until), без (without), з/із (from/out of), для (for), після (after), біля (near).

exercises.explanation: До + genitive: школа → школи.
Без requires the genitive case: молоко → молока.
Від requires genitive: бабуся → бабусі. Feminine nouns ending in -я change to -і.
Для requires the genitive case: мама → мами.

exercises.prompt: Match each preposition phrase to its correct form:
exercises.explanation: Each preposition requires the genitive: друг → друга, місто → міста, сіль → солі, сестра → сестри, урок → уроку.

exercises.prompt: Put the noun in the correct case after the preposition:
exercises.explanation: Після requires genitive: робота → роботи.
З (from) requires the genitive case: магазин → магазину.
Біля + genitive: парк → парку (inanimate location noun takes -у).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear:
exercises.explanation: До + genitive: лікар → лікаря. Masculine animate nouns take -а/-я.

exercises.hint: Із + genitive; дім is a special noun.
exercises.explanation: Дім → дому in the genitive. Із requires the genitive case.

sections.title: Genitive with Numbers
sections.explanation: Numbers affect the case of the noun: 1 = nominative singular; 2, 3, 4 = genitive singular; 5-20 and compound numbers ending in 5-9, 0 = genitive plural. This is one of the trickiest parts of Ukrainian grammar.

exercises.explanation: After 2, 3, 4 we use the nominative plural (which looks like genitive singular for masculine): три студенти.
After два/дві, три, чотири, the noun takes the genitive singular form: година → години.
After 5 and above, the noun takes the genitive plural: день → днів.

exercises.hint: 5+ takes genitive plural; feminine nouns drop the ending.
exercises.explanation: Хвилина → хвилин (genitive plural). Feminine nouns ending in -а drop the -а and may have zero ending.
After три, the noun takes the genitive singular: собака → собаки.

exercises.prompt: Use the correct form after the number:
exercises.explanation: Місяць → місяців (genitive plural). After 5+, masculine nouns often take -ів.

exercises.prompt: Match each number phrase to the correct noun form:
exercises.explanation: 1 = nominative; 2-4 = genitive singular (or nom. plural for some); 5+ = genitive plural.
Compound numbers ending in 1 (двадцять одна) use the nominative singular: гривня.
П'ять requires the genitive plural: яблуко → яблук.

exercises.prompt: Complete the noun form for each number:
exercises.explanation: Рік: 2-4 → роки, 5+ → років. Яблуко: 2-4 → яблука (gen. sg.), 5+ → яблук (gen. pl.). Гривня: 2-4 → гривні (gen. sg.).
